notes: Figure AI's initial institutional raise to fund Figure 01 development and initial team build-out.

Landmark round featuring the most prominent AI and tech investors in the world. Concurrent with OpenAI partnership for humanoid AI model development. Microsoft and Nvidia participation reflects compute infrastructure interest.
Largest single funding round in humanoid robotics history. Concurrent with Figure 03 mass production announcement. BMW participation as strategic investor validates commercial deployment thesis.
OpenAI's first direct investment in a robotics hardware company, signaling the AI lab's intent to influence physical AI development.
Funds NEO bipedal humanoid development and home trial program expansion. EQT Ventures' lead represents growing European institutional interest in humanoid robotics.

Amazon's $150M investment is both strategic capital and a commercial deployment commitment. Agility opened a RoboFab manufacturing facility in Salem, OR with this funding to produce Digit units at scale.
Funds development of Phoenix Gen 5-6 and Carbon AI cognitive system. Accenture's participation reflects enterprise services interest in humanoid deployment integration.

Magna International's lead investment is strategically significant — as one of the world's largest Tier-1 automotive suppliers, Magna provides both capital and a deployment pathway into automotive manufacturing.
Google's lead investment reflects Alphabet's broad physical AI strategy. Apptronik will integrate with Google DeepMind's robotics research for Apollo AI model development.
Europe's largest humanoid Series A at the time, anchored by German tech investors. Positions NEURA as Europe's champion in the race to build cognitive humanoids.
NEA's US participation signals transatlantic investor interest in NEURA's cognitive humanoid approach. Funds 4NE-1 pilot programs and manufacturing scale-up.

One of the largest seed rounds in history, reflecting the caliber of the founding team (Levine, Finn, Hausman) and the transformative potential of foundation models for robotics. This round price is equivalent to the Series B of most companies.
Relatively modest Series A reflects Pi's capital-light model — the company licenses AI models rather than manufacturing hardware. Funds π0.2 model development and platform integrations.

Largest pre-revenue Series A in robotics AI history at the time. Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta's CMU pedigree and early results on the Skild Brain — an omni-bodied foundation model that controls any robot form factor without body-specific fine-tuning — attracted Lightspeed, SoftBank, Coatue, and Amazon among 10+ investors.

Skild triples its valuation in under a year. SoftBank doubles down as lead, joined by Nvidia (NVentures) whose GR00T platform now offers Skild Brain as an integration option. Samsung and LG's participation reflects Korean OEM interest in licensing a universal robot AI for consumer humanoid products.

The largest single funding round in robot AI history, vaulting Skild to a $14B valuation — nearly matching Physical Intelligence's $5.6B combined. Macquarie Group's infrastructure-focused participation signals investor conviction that robot foundation models will become utility-scale infrastructure. All major Series A/B investors including Sequoia, Coatue, Felicis, Samsung, LG, Salesforce Ventures, and CommonSpirit Health doubled down.

Unitree's B round reflects strong domestic Chinese investor conviction in the company's price-disruptive strategy. Meituan's involvement hints at potential deployment in food delivery and logistics automation.
Zhangjiang Hi-Tech's participation reflects the Shanghai city government's strategic interest in building a local humanoid champion. Agibot is headquartered in Zhangjiang Hi-Tech Park.
Agibot achieves unicorn status with its Series B, driven by scale-up of teleoperation data infrastructure and expanded logistics pilot deployments.
Kepler's heavy-payload differentiation attracted manufacturing-focused investors. Suzhou municipal government co-invested through Oriza to secure local manufacturing commitment.
Clone has operated largely bootstrapped with angel backing, relying on community support and pre-orders to fund R&D. Unconventional funding approach reflects founder philosophy of building in public.
Lux Capital's participation represents the first institutional investment in Clone, attracted by the Myofiber breakthrough and viral P1 demonstration.

Hyundai acquired an 80% controlling stake from SoftBank, which had acquired the company from Google in 2017. The deal gave Boston Dynamics independence to pursue commercial products while accessing Hyundai's manufacturing expertise and factory deployment sites.
Funds GR-1 commercial launch and GR2 development. Fourier's exoskeleton revenue provides financial stability that pure-play humanoid companies lack.
Tencent's lead investment connects Astribot to one of China's largest technology ecosystems and potential consumer deployment channels.
Funds S1 pilot program expansion and hospitality market development. Yunfeng Capital (Jack Ma's fund) participation signals broad Chinese tech establishment support.
BMW's strategic investment bridges the Series B and C rounds, deepening the operational partnership at Spartanburg and providing European automotive channel validation.
Bell Canada's participation reflects growing Canadian corporate interest in enterprise robotics. Funds Phoenix Gen 8 development and expanded RaaS deployment program.
NASA SBIR Phase II grant funding Apollo development through the NASA Johnson Space Center partnership. Critical early non-dilutive capital that funded actuator technology development.

Landmark round featuring all three major German automotive OEMs as investors, validating NEURA's industrial humanoid thesis and providing manufacturing deployment pathways. NEURA becomes Europe's first humanoid unicorn.

Massive extension to Apptronik's Series A brings total raised to over $870M. The round is earmarked for Apollo production scale-up at their Austin, Texas facility and expansion of commercial deployment programs with GXO Logistics, Mercedes-Benz, and other enterprise partners.

Europe's largest-ever humanoid robotics funding round, led by crypto infrastructure giant Tether making its first major robotics bet. The round propels NEURA to a ~$4B valuation and funds mass production of the MAiRA and 4NE-1 cognitive humanoids at a new Reutlingen mega-factory. CEO David Reger cites 'the convergence of cognitive AI and physical embodiment' as the defining investment thesis.

One of the largest humanoid raises in China's history, anchored by two of the country's most powerful tech platforms. Alibaba's participation opens doors to deployment across its vast logistics network, while Meituan integration could enable last-mile delivery applications. Galbot's full-body dexterous manipulation technology is the primary investor draw.

Sunday achieves unicorn status with its Series B, reaching a $1.15B valuation on the strength of its household humanoid assistant product. The company has pivoted from pure-industrial to consumer-plus-commercial, targeting the home care and elderly assistance markets in Japan, South Korea, and China. Hillhouse's lead reflects conviction that the consumer humanoid market will outpace industrial deployment by 2028.

PI's Series B is led by CapitalG — Alphabet's growth fund — making it Google's largest robotics foundation model bet. The $5.6B valuation comes despite PI being pre-revenue, reflecting investor conviction in the π0 and π0.5 VLA models as the operating system for the coming wave of commercial humanoids. Bond, Redpoint, and Sequoia joined. PI open-sourced π0 in February 2025, growing the developer ecosystem and validating the platform strategy.

Samsung's lead investment reflects South Korean strategic interest in humanoid platforms as a potential future product category. The round funds scale-up of the NEO Beta home humanoid trial program to 500 households across Norway, the US, and Japan — the largest real-world home humanoid deployment to date.

Unitree's Series C double its valuation, driven by exceptional revenue from the G1 and H1 humanoid platforms. The company shipped over 3,000 robots in 2025 — more than any other humanoid manufacturer globally — making it the first humanoid robotics company to reach meaningful commercial scale.

Figure's Series D comes on the heels of confirming 100+ Figure 02 units deployed at BMW Spartanburg. Microsoft deepens its investment to secure Figure as a preferred Azure and Azure AI customer, while Nvidia's participation reflects the company's continued push to become the dominant compute platform for humanoid inference.

Founded by ex-Nvidia researchers who built Isaac Gym (David Hoeller) and ETH Zurich specialists, Flexion is building an intelligence layer for humanoid robots using reinforcement learning and transformer-based whole-body control trained primarily on synthetic simulation data. NVentures' lead participation reflects Nvidia's strategy of backing pure-play robot AI software companies as complements to its GR00T and Isaac platforms. Flexion's hardware-agnostic licensing model — software charged per robot per year — is the clearest attempt yet to create an 'Android of humanoid robotics.'

Founded by Nic Radford (ex-NASA Johnson Space Center, Toyota Research Institute) and Jerry Pratt (ex-Florida Human & Machine Cognition Institute), Persona AI targets the most demanding physical environments — shipbuilding, energy infrastructure, and construction — where current humanoids are underpowered. A strategic investment from POSCO Group (South Korea's largest steelmaker) provides both capital and a direct deployment pathway into heavy industrial facilities. The $27M oversubscribed pre-seed, anchored by Mirae Asset Group's Korean institutional backing, signals crossover investor interest in purpose-built industrial humanoids.

Dyna Robotics builds DYNA-1, a robotic foundation model targeting commercial service environments — hotels, restaurants, laundromats, gyms. NVentures (Nvidia), Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund, Samsung Next, LG Technology Ventures, and Salesforce Ventures all participated, reflecting the breadth of industries betting on service robotics AI. The company claims 99%+ task success over 24-hour continuous operation. While not exclusively humanoid hardware, Dyna's foundation model approach positions it as infrastructure for the next wave of commercial humanoid deployments.
